summary: Frontend-only Expo/React Native testing for Brainrot RPG. Tested NEW features: TUT spawn (PASS), MC Lock for Angel Tung (PASS), Elevation cliff block message (PASS), gate popup testIDs present, REPLAY HELP button present, soul cursor wired. CRITICAL BLOCKER: The TUT Sanctuary (map 14) is unbeatable — player gets stuck on the mid plateau due to missing ramp tiles, which blocks every downstream flow (realm entry, tutorial overlay, layer 1 gate, combat). Recommend hot-fix before re-test. Title→New Game→Slot pick→TUT spawn confirmed. MC lock checks all pass. Elevation block flash works on first wrong climb.

ui_bugs.issue: Player can climb to the MID plateau via ramp (5,8) but CANNOT leave it. To progress north, the path is (5,6 h=1) → (5,5 h=0) — diff=1 needing a ramp at (5,5) or (5,6); neither exists. Also (5,4 h=0) → (5,3 h=2) — diff=2, which heightAllowsStep blocks regardless of ramp because of the abs(h1-h2)>1 short-circuit. Result: puzzle (5,2), exit (5,1) and tutorial completion are unreachable. The entire downstream game (realm, gates, combat) cannot be exercised by a new player.

Ramp tiles in RAMPS_BY_MAP are not also entered into HEIGHT_BY_MAP, and heightAllowsStep blocks any move where |h1-h2|>1 BEFORE checking ramps. The ramp's `height` field is unused. This breaks the 2-tier design: stepping from floor (h=0) onto top-plateau (h=2) is never allowed. Reference: /app/frontend/app/index.tsx:34-40 and /app/frontend/src/game/data.ts:498-534.

integration_issues.issue: Could not be tested end-to-end because TUT is unbeatable. completeTutorial / brainrot_tutorial_seen_v1 / TUTORIAL_STEPS overlay flow remains unverified.

Could not be reached because player never warps to realm. Code path (canEnterLayer1 + setGatePopup with testID gate-popup / gate-close) is wired correctly in index.tsx:259-275 but needs TUT bug fixed before retest.

integration_issues.flow: Combat + speech bubble + reaction + damage cap
integration_issues.issue: Could not be reached due to TUT block. combat-screen, enemy-bubble, reaction-panel, react-success/fail testIDs exist; capDmg=200 implemented in engine.ts:316-317. Requires retest after TUT fix.

issues: Players have no in-game hint that they are stuck — the only feedback is '* The cliff is too steep. Walk to a ramp.' which the player will see infinitely with no recourse.
Consider also adding sub-ramps within the central column (e.g. ramp tiles at (5,5) and (5,3) so the floor strip rows 4-5 actually bridges the two plateaus).
MC lock implementation is clean. Locked note text and ♥ MC badge render correctly; EQUIP/EVOLVE/TRAIT all hidden as required.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Likely from react-native-safe-area-context / RN web. Non-blocking but worth migrating once. Reference: /app/frontend/app/index.tsx (multiple Pressable / View usages).

action_items: FIX (CRITICAL): TUT Sanctuary (map id=14) cannot be completed because of missing ramps between mid and top plateaus. Two options:

Option A (data fix): Add ramp tiles at (5,5) and (5,3) in RAMPS_BY_MAP[14] AND give them a height entry so they sit between floor and plateau. Specifically: add HEIGHT_BY_MAP[14] entries {(5,5): h=1} and {(5,3): h=2} (so each ramp bridges exactly 1 tier).

Option B (logic fix): Update heightAllowsStep so that when stepping onto/off a ramp, the ramp's declared `height` overrides getHeight. Pseudo: if isRamp(to), treat h2 := rampHeight - 1 (climbing); if isRamp(from), treat h1 := rampHeight - 1. This decouples data from code.
After TUT fix, re-run the full priority flow (TUT → realm → tutorial overlay → MC lock retest → gate popup → combat reaction).
Verify Combat damage cap by reading combat-log lines: assert no `→ Ndmg` where N > 200.
Verify soul cursor prefix: cmd-fight default starts with '♥ ' (PASS in current screenshot — see CONSOLE log).
Optional: replace deprecated `props.pointerEvents` usages with `style.pointerEvents` to clear the console warning.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/app/index.tsx is 1389 lines — consider splitting scenes (TitleScene, InventoryScene, CombatScene, PuzzleScene, TutorialOverlay) into separate files for maintainability.
engine.ts:387 — stepOnTrap message still says 'Angel Sahur takes ... dmg' but the MC has been renamed to 'Angel Tung'. Cosmetic but should be updated for consistency.
TUTORIAL_STEPS body in index.tsx:1160 says 'You play Angel Sahur.' — should be 'Angel Tung' to match the renamed MC.
heightAllowsStep does not respect a ramp's declared `height`, which is the root cause of the TUT block. The data and logic are out of sync.

success_rate.frontend: Approx 40% — features that could be validated PASS; ~60% of the new-feature surface area is gated behind the TUT block.

seed_data_creation: None (offline game, used in-game New Game → Slot 1).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Once TUT ramps/heights are fixed, run the same Playwright flow used here. Specifically validate: (1) walk path (5,12)→(5,1) via ramps reaches puzzle at (5,2); (2) answering puzzle choice 1 ('A gacha banner') solves and grants +50 coins; (3) stepping on (5,1) triggers completeTutorial → realm map id=1 at PLAYER_DEFAULT_REALM, and tutorial-overlay appears once; (4) on realm, no ATK equipped + step on (6,15) shows gate-popup with title 'THE STAIR REJECTS YOU' and a working gate-close; (5) equipping any ATK then stepping on stair enters layer map 101; (6) combat shows enemy-bubble + after FIGHT shows reaction-panel; (7) every damage line in combat-log has number ≤ 200.
rca_of_issue: Root cause: heightAllowsStep(from, to) in /app/frontend/app/index.tsx:34-40 reads both tiles' heights from HEIGHT_BY_MAP, then enforces abs(h1-h2)<=1 BEFORE consulting RAMPS_BY_MAP. Ramp tiles in TUT (cols 5 row 8 and 5 row 4) are not present in HEIGHT_BY_MAP[14], so their getHeight() returns 0. Consequence: (a) ramp at (5,4) cannot bridge floor→top-plateau (height diff 2); (b) mid-plateau (rows 6-7, h=1) has no descending ramp at its northern edge, trapping the player. Reproduction: start a new game; from default spawn (5,12) walk up via (5,8) ramp; once on mid plateau press ↑ — flash 'The cliff is too steep.' permanently. Mitigation: see action_items 'Option A' (data fix) or 'Option B' (engine fix).
